text:
The Devil's Nest is a large pub located in the "bad side" of Dublith. This place is notable for being the headquarters of Greed and his chimera gang of outcast mercenaries. From here, Greed keeps a close ear on all the goings of the "business underworld" throughout Amestris.
The Devil's Nest itself seems to be connected to a series of maze-like underground escape routes, mostly ending in the large sewer network below.

Members

Greed's faction mainly comprises of humanoid chimera. The named members are as follows:
Greed
Martel
Bido
Roa
Dolcetto
Ulchi
Zolf J. Kimbley

Manga/2009 Anime

Shortly after Edward Elric and Izumi Curtis travel into the pub in search of a captured Alphonse, the place is raided by King Bradley and his military forces. Greed was captured and later executed by Father, and nearly all the chimera in his faction were slaughtered by the soldiers. Bido is ultimately the only chimera to escape the slaughter and survive, but even he is eventually killed much later by the new reincarnated version of Greed.

While the Devil's Nest is long gone by the time Greed is reincarnated, he does end up forming a new group of sorts that could be considered a successor to his original, comprised of himself, the chimeras Heinkel and Darius, and Edward Elric.

2003 Anime

The Devil's Nest plays a rather similar role in the 2003 anime, although notably, Frank Archer, a character exclusive to that adaptation, leads the raid on the Devil's Nest rather than King Bradley. Zolf J. Kimbley is also present due to having joined forces with Greed, ultimately being the one to betray him and give away the location of his hideout.

Additionally, Bido's role is cut short due to dying during the slaughter. Instead, Alphonse manages to successfully escape with Martel, who ends up playing a larger role. Despite surviving Archer's assault, though, Martel ends up still being killed much later by King Bradley while hiding in Alphonse's armour, in a very similar way to the original story.

Trivia

During the fight between Ed and Greed, in one of the frames, a bottle of liquor labeled Stray Dog can be seen. This is a reference to Hiromu Arakawa's earlier manga series of the same name.
